title: コマンド/vote が正常に作動しているか徹底的に調査してください
summary_text: 1. **Critical: runtime hardening is not reproducible from the source repo as-is.** `consensus-vote.js` in source imports new libs ([consensus-vote.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L29)) but key files are still untracked in git (`safe-env.js`, `consensus-logic.js`, `provider-preflight.js`), and all new `/vote` tests are also untracked. This creates a deploy/regression risk where git-based rollout loses current runtime behavior. 2. **High: active runtime/source drift remains on core `/vote` behavior and guardrails.** Runtime has JSON-first vote parsing while source is still older parse logic ([runtime consensus-vote.js](/Users/masayuki/.claude/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L296), [source consensus-vote.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/consensus-vote.js#L296)). Source `auto-vote.sh` still lacks `FAIL_OPEN` initialization ([source auto-vote.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/hooks/auto-vote.sh#L24), [runtime auto-vote.sh](/Users/masayuki/.claude/hooks/auto-vote.sh#L24)). `quality-gates.json` and `settings.json` are materially different (including `dangerouslySkipPermissions`, PreToolUse matcher scope, and hook graph): [source settings.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/settings.json#L33), [runtime settings.json](/Users/masayuki/.claude/settings.json#L417). 3. **High: preflight improved, but still not “realistic enough” for provider readiness.** Current preflight checks binary/credentials/DNS ([provider-preflight.js](/Users/masayuki/.claude/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/provider-preflight.js#L62)) but does not perform a real auth/API probe. In runtime smoke (`--no-claude`), codex was excluded, but glm/gemini passed preflight then both immediately `ABSTAIN`, ending `BLOCKED` with `degradedMode: true`. This indicates false-positive availability. 4. **Medium: test coverage exists but is not operationally protective yet.** I ran and passed: - [test-auto-vote-verdicts.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/tests/test-auto-vote-verdicts.sh) - [consensus-config.test.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/hooks/quality-gates/tests/consensus-config.test.js) - [consensus-logic.test.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/skills/orchestra-delegator/tests/consensus-logic.test.js) - [provider-preflight.test.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config/skills/orchestra-delegator/tests/provider-preflight.test.js) But these tests are untracked and not wired into a repo test pipeline, so they do not prevent regressions. 5. **Medium: one fail-open contradiction remains in runtime policy.** If consensus script is missing, `auto-vote.sh` allows operation (`exit 0`) ([auto-vote.sh](/Users/masayuki/.claude/hooks/auto-vote.sh#L183)). That conflicts with the otherwise fail-closed posture for inconclusive votes. No files were edited.
